use_cases: Primary action trigger in web applications with Material Design aesthetics
Form submission buttons with tactile audio feedback on touch devices
Interactive call-to-action elements with MD3 ripple physics animations
Navigation action triggers with hover and press state visualization
Accessible button component with keyboard navigation support via Radix UI Slot
ai_summary: A Material Design 3 button component implementing MD3 physics-based ripple effects with dynamic audio generation. Features CVA-based variant system, touch-optimized interactions with delay handling, and a sophisticated state machine for ripple lifecycle management. The component uses Radix UI Slot for composition flexibility and generates Web Audio API tactile pop sounds on interaction.
